subject: [PATCH 0/8] dax/kmem: atomic whole-device hotplug via sysfs

reviews.author: Gregory Price
reviews.summary: Raised concerns about userland seeing stale memory blocks during hotplug operations, but considers it a non-issue for udev. Suggested that the patch should handle offline and online races cleanly.
reviews.raw_body:
On Mon, Apr 13, 2026 at 09:40:49PM +0200, David Hildenbrand (Arm) wrote:
> > 
> > I don't think this is a very large lift, just a slightly larger hotplug
> > locking scope.  But then - the per-range thing in this set should just
> > work, so let me know if it's worth the extra churn.
> 
> Well, you can rather cleanly undo the operation if any offlining fails.
> If that's not a requirement, then no need to change it!
> 

It's not a requirement for me, but maybe it makes sense that

   echo unplug > dax0.0/hotplug

Either fully succeeds or fully rolls back.  Otherwise `cat hotplug`
would have to report "\_(\u30c4)_/".

So makes sense, i'll come back around on this.

~Gregory

reviews.author: David Hildenbrand (Arm)
reviews.summary: Agreed with Gregory's assessment, suggesting that the patch can cleanly undo operations if offlining fails. Proposed adding a requirement for full success or rollback in hotplug operations.

reviews.author: Gregory Price
reviews.summary: Considered David's suggestion, but decided to revisit the requirement for full success or rollback in hotplug operations.
reviews.raw_body:

2026-04-13.patch_summary: Atomic whole-device hotplug via sysfs, addressing offline and online races.
